Include hours worked by salaried, hourly, part-time and contract workers including temporary help services workers under your supervision, even if they were not under your direct employment. Do not include vacation, sick leave, holidays, or any other non-work time, even if employees were paid for it. If your establishment keeps records of only the hours paid or if you have employees who are not paid by the hour, please estimate the hours that the employees actually worked. If this number isn't available, an estimate is still needed.
Please enter the total number of hours worked by all employees during the given year. Please round your answer to the next highest whole number.
Defined as a work accident or occupational event that causes the death of the worker.
Please enter the total number of work related fatalities for the given year.

The permanent reduction of a worker’s physical or mental capacity due to the loss or paralysis of any organ or function, preventing them from performing their usual work but not all types of work.\n\nGeneral Regulation of the Social Security Law (Decree No. 975), Article 74(b)
Please enter the number of partial permanent disability incidents for the given year.

The total and permanent loss of physical or mental capacity that makes it impossible for the worker to perform any work for the rest of their life.\n\nGeneral Regulation of the Social Security Law (Decree No. 975), Article 74(c)
Please enter the number of total permanent disability incidents for the given year.

### High Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *HIGHER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

### Low Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *LOWER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time and part-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

A pathological condition caused by continuous exposure to factors arising from or related to the work performed, which results in a functional disorder or incapacity (temporary or permanent). Ley de Seguridad Social (Decreto N° 974), Art. 64
Please enter the total number of occupational disease cases recorded during the reporting year.

Days away from work are counted from the first full day after the accident or diagnosis of an occupational disease until the day the worker is able to return to regular duties. Absences resulting from work-related accidents or occupational diseases must be recorded as per national labor reporting requirements.\n\nLey de Seguridad Social (Decreto N° 974), Art. 63
Please enter the total number of days away from work.

A very serious accident (accidente muy grave) is one that causes the worker to be absent from work for more than 26 consecutive weeks and results in very severe or multiple injuries, such as multiple fractures, amputations, or poly-trauma.\n\nREGLAMENTO DE LA LEY GENERAL DE HIGIENE Y SEGURIDAD DEL TRABAJO. Art 14.
Please enter the total number of very serious occupational accidents recorded during the reporting year (more than 26 weeks of absence).

A serious accident (accidente grave) causes a worker to miss 8 or more days of work and involves serious injuries such as fractures, severe burns, or amputations.\n\nREGLAMENTO DE LA LEY GENERAL DE HIGIENE Y SEGURIDAD DEL TRABAJO. Art 13.
Please enter the total number of serious occupational accidents recorded during the reporting year (8 days to 26 weeks of absence).

A lost-time accident (accidente leve con baja) is one that causes the worker to miss at least one day and up to seven days of work due to injuries requiring medical care or rest.\n\nREGLAMENTO DE LA LEY GENERAL DE HIGIENE Y SEGURIDAD DEL TRABAJO. Art 12.
Please enter the total number of lost-time occupational accidents (1 to 7 days of absence) recorded during the reporting year.

A dangerous occurrence is any incident or situation at the workplace that, without causing injury to workers, represents a serious and imminent risk to their safety or health.\n\nLey de Seguridad Social (Decreto N° 974), Art. 41 (e).
Please enter the total number of dangerous occurrences recorded during the reporting year.
